rules.limit: Verification required via GSFA Online Database
rules.sourceTitle: Food Additives DB (GSFA Online) | CODEXALIMENTARIUS FAO-WHO
rules.notes: The Codex General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA, Codex STAN 192-1995) sets forth the conditions under which permitted food additives may be used in all foods. Users are encouraged to consult the database and its Preamble.
rules.evidenceQuote: The 'Codex General Standard for Food Additives' (GSFA, Codex STAN 192-1995) sets forth the conditions under which permitted food additives may be used in all foods

rules.limit: Verification required via Codex General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995)
rules.notes: Codex establishes maximum levels (MLs) and guideline levels for contaminants and naturally occurring toxicants in food and feed. These levels are legally permitted concentrations.
rules.evidenceQuote: The Codex maximum level (ML) for a contaminant in a food or feed commodity is the maximum concentration of that substance recommended by the Codex Alimentarius Commission to be legally permitted in that commodity.

rules.limit: Follow Code of Practice for the Prevention and Reduction of Mycotoxins in Spices
rules.notes: This Code of Practice provides guidance for the prevention and reduction of mycotoxin contamination specifically in spices.
rules.evidenceQuote: CXC 78-2017 Code of Practice for the Prevention and Reduction of Mycotoxins in Spices

Regulations for food additives, including permitted uses and limits, are established by MHLW.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food Additives

rules.sourceTitle: Specifications and Standards for Food, Food Additives, etc. Under The Food Sanitation Act
rules.notes: This document contains detailed specifications and standards for various aspects of food, including permitted additives, contaminant limits, and potentially microbiological criteria. Verification is required.
rules.evidenceQuote: Specifications and Standards for Food, Food Additives, etc. Under The Food Sanitation Act

rules.notes: The Positive List System specifies maximum residue limits for agricultural chemicals in food.
rules.evidenceQuote: Positive List System for Agricultural Chemical Residues in Food

rules.notes: Specific guidelines for handling food contaminated by radioactivity are issued by MHLW.
rules.evidenceQuote: Handling of food contaminated by radioactivity

rules.limit: Compliance with Food Safety Basic Law
rules.notes: This law establishes the basic principles for ensuring food safety in Japan. Compliance with its principles and derived regulations is required.
rules.evidenceQuote: The Food Safety Basic Law

rules.notes: This foundational law governs all aspects of food sanitation in Japan, including standards for ingredients, additives, contaminants, and labeling. Specific requirements must be verified.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food Sanitation Law

rules.parameter: Nutrition Labeling, Health Claims, Special Dietary Uses
rules.notes: Specific requirements for labeling of nutrition, health claims, and food for special dietary uses must be verified.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food with Health Claims, Food for Special Dietary Uses, and Nutrition Labeling

rules.sourceTitle: Regional grouping - use GCC/GSO plus country-specific authorities
rules.notes: Automated extraction failed: {"error":{"code":503,"message":"This model is currently experiencing high demand. Spikes in demand are usually temporary. Please try again later.","status":"UNAVAILABLE"}}

rules.limit: Use calculation tool for permitted levels
rules.notes: Applies if two or more Class II chemical preservatives (e.g., sorbic acid or benzoic acid) are used.
rules.evidenceQuote: If two or more Class II chemical preservatives are to be used in the food (e.g. sorbic acid or benzoic acid), use the Calculation Tool for Class II Chemical Preservatives to calculate the permitted levels that should be used.

rules.foodCategory: seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, ready-to-eat snacks
rules.notes: Different limits apply depending on the additive and food product type. Refer to 'Guidance Information on Requirements for Use of Food Additives' and SFA's Legislation.
rules.evidenceQuote: Different limits apply, depending on the additive and the type of food products. Use the Guidance Information on Requirements for Use of Food Additives to learn more about the different types of additives and the corresponding regulations that impose the limits.

rules.foodCategory: seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, ready-to-eat snacks
rules.limit: Must be assessed for safety by SFA
rules.notes: Applies to all food sold in Singapore.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food additives must be assessed for safety by SFA before they can be used in food sold in Singapore.

rules.foodCategory: seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, ready-to-eat snacks
rules.notes: Verification required using the Food Additives Search tool or the List of Permitted Food Additives.
rules.evidenceQuote: Only food additives that have been assessed by SFA are permitted for use in food products sold in Singapore.

rules.foodCategory: seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, ready-to-eat snacks
rules.notes: An application can be submitted to SFA to evaluate the use of an unpermitted additive.
rules.evidenceQuote: If your food product contains an additive that is not permitted, do not import or sell it.

rules.foodCategory: seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, ready-to-eat snacks
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Refer to SFA documents for Microbiological standards, Heavy metals, Mycotoxins and Marine biotoxins, Veterinary drug residues, Pesticide residues, and Other incidental constituents.
rules.evidenceQuote: There are various limits imposed on different types of contaminants or incidental constituents in food. For more details, please refer to the relevant documents below: Microbiological standards [...] Heavy metals [...] Mycotoxins and Marine biotoxins [...] Veterinary drug residues [...] Pesticide residues [...] Other incidental constituents

rules.foodCategory: seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, ready-to-eat snacks
rules.limit: Must be within SFA’s regulatory limits
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Applies to all food sold in Singapore.
rules.evidenceQuote: Contaminants (also known as incidental constituents) in food sold in Singapore must be within SFA’s regulatory limits.

rules.foodCategory: seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, ready-to-eat snacks
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Food businesses must ensure contaminants are within specified limits based on laboratory tests.
rules.evidenceQuote: If your food or food products contain contaminants or incidental constituents that exceed the regulatory limits, do not import or sell them.

rules.foodCategory: meat and food derived from meat
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Prohibits import or sale of meat and any food derived from meat containing these specific compounds.
rules.evidenceQuote: Regulation 33 of the Food Regulations prohibits the import or sale of meat and any food derived from meat which contains residues of the following compounds: diethylstibestrol [...] hexoestrol [...] dienoestrol

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: SFA has established a list of maximum residue limits (MRLs) for other veterinary drug residues in various food commodities. These MRLs can be downloaded from the SFA website.
rules.evidenceQuote: SFA has also assessed the risk of the presence of other veterinary drug residues in various food commodities, and has established a list of maximum residue limits (MRLs) for veterinary drug residues for compliance by food businesses with effect from 1 May 2020.

rules.limit: Must be declared on food labels
rules.notes: Applies to prepacked foods for sale in Singapore.
rules.evidenceQuote: All ingredients and additives used in food products must be declared in list of ingredients on food labels.

rules.foodCategory: ready-to-eat snacks, seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Refer to SFA documents for microbiological standards for ready-to-eat food and non-ready-to-eat food.
rules.evidenceQuote: Microbiological standards Ready-to-eat food Non-ready-to-eat food (till 3 Mar 2024) Non-ready-to-eat food (from 4 Mar 2024)

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Applies to Ready-to-eat (RTE) food.
rules.evidenceQuote: Ready-to-eat (RTE) food are not required to be tested for total plate count (TPC).

rules.parameter: Toxic substances and veterinary drug residues

rules.notes: General requirements for food and nutrition labeling, including allergen declarations and sodium content, must be verified.

rules.limit: Prohibited from production, import, or sale
